commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `resolveCronPayloadOutcome` so recovered `isError` payloads can be nonfatal when later output, terminal assistant-visible text, or presentation-warning recovery proves the run recovered, and adds focused resolver regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current `main` has a resolver test where all payloads are `isError: true` and final assistant text says recovery succeeded, yet the helper still selects the last error, which `finalizeCronRun` maps to `status: error`.
commands.review_followup: Do not queue an automated repair: the open PR is the implementation path, but it needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the cron status compatibility change before merge.

target.title: fix(cron): respect recovered tool errors
target.body:
## Summary

- treat `isError` payloads as fatal only when they are not recovered by later successful output or terminal assistant-visible text
- keep typed fatal signals (`meta.error`, fatal `failureSignal`, denial markers) fatal even when assistant text is present
- add recovery metadata and regression coverage for all-error payloads followed by recovered final assistant output

## Context

Fixes #74807.
Relates #81514.
Alternative to #74840, which still lets the last error payload override a recovered final assistant result in some cron runs.

## Test plan

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.meta-error-status.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
target.author: elarwei001
